channel.title: Muk Buddy Blog
channel.description: Field-tested tips and cost breakdowns for wet/dry vacuum users.

item.title: Why Your Shop Vac Keeps Clogging on Drywall Dust (and How to Actually Stop It)
item.description: Drywall dust kills shop vac suction in minutes. Here's exactly why it happens — and the fix that keeps your filter breathing and your motor alive.

item.title: Disposable Shop Vac Bags Don't Wear Out — They Fail Mid-Job
item.description: A disposable vac bag doesn't fade gracefully — it rips or blows out and dumps your whole load back onto the floor. Here's why it happens and how to stop it.

item.title: Why Your Shop Vac Lost Its Suction (and How to Get It Back)
item.description: Shop vac barely pulling air? Suction loss almost always traces back to one of four things. Here's how to find it fast — and keep it from coming back.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Shop Vac Downtime on a Jobsite
item.description: Every time the crew stops to clean a filter, swap a bag, or fight weak suction, you're paying for it. Here's the real math on shop vac downtime — and how to kill it.

item.title: Running Your Shop Vac With No Bag? Here's What It's Really Costing You
item.description: Going bagless feels free. On fine jobsite dust it's the fastest way to kill your filter and your motor. Here's the real cost — and when bagless is actually fine.

item.title: 10 Reasons Contractors Are Switching to a Reusable Shop Vac Bag
item.description: From suction loss to blowouts to the money you burn on disposables — here are the 10 real reasons working crews are dropping throwaway wet/dry vac bags for good.

item.title: Disposable shop vac bags vs. reusable: the real cost over a year
item.description: Disposable wet/dry vac bags feel cheap at the register and brutal at year-end. Here's the real math — and what a reusable bag actually saves a working crew.
